sources.notes: Count replacements of ? in a binary pattern so every length-k substring has the leftmost bit as strict majority.
Blog solution observes the transition of the signed count between adjacent windows and derives period k-1 after the first switch.

annotation.summary: Count binary completions such that in every length-k window, the first bit occurs more often than the other bit.
annotation.constraints: n totals 1e5 and k is odd, so O(n) or O(n log n) counting over forced periodic classes is needed.
annotation.core_idea: For a window, track d = #1 - #0. When moving one position right, d changes by -2, 0, or +2 depending on the removed left bit and new right bit. A valid window whose left bit changes from 1 to 0 forces the previous signed count to be 1, the next to be -1, and the new entering bit to be 0; continuing this shows that after the first change, the suffix is periodic with period k-1. Enumerate the prefix length of the initial run, impose the resulting periodic constraints, and count choices with the required balance of (k-1)/2 zeros/ones in the boundary block.
annotation.complexity: O(n) to O(n log n) depending on prefix-count implementation.
tricks: first change forces a period k-1 suffix

solution_variants.name: periodic-first-switch-count
solution_variants.summary: Try the initial bit and first switch position, propagate period k-1 constraints, and count balanced assignments compatible with the pattern.

tags.evidence: The final answer counts assignments of constrained residue classes and balanced blocks.
The object is a constrained binary string with wildcard pattern matching.
Most pitfalls are in propagating forced positions, checking ? compatibility, and handling symmetric initial bit cases.
